```{r, echo=T}
                    #race_var + #race removed because children added
                    children, #children included because was barely significant when income aggregated
                 family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                 data = hh_df)
summary(income.hts.2b)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_inc_change)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var +
                  all_young_adults +
                  # race_var +
                  children,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(income.hts.2b)

confint(income.hts.2b) #confidence intervals
# coef(income.hts.2b) #log odds
exp(coef(income.hts.2b)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(income.hts.2b)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(income.hts.2b)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_income.2b <- (income.hts.2b$null.deviance - income.hts.2b$deviance) / income.hts.2b$null.deviance
pseudoR2_income.2b
```

#### 3e. Multivariate analyses refined - Income Change

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3c). This model includes the expanded (non-aggregated) income variable. It does not include race and does not include families and children. 

```{r, echo=T}
income.hts.2c<-glm(disp_inc_change~1 +
                    income_var +
                    all_young_adults, #+
                    #race_var + #race removed because wasn't significant when race and income were aggregated
                    # children, #children included because was barely significant when income aggregated
                 family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                 data = hh_df)
summary(income.hts.2c)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_inc_change)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var +
                  all_young_adults, #+
                  # race_var +
                  # children,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(income.hts.2c)

confint(income.hts.2c) #confidence intervals
# coef(income.hts.2c) #log odds
exp(coef(income.hts.2c)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(income.hts.2c)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(income.hts.2c)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_income.2c <- (income.hts.2c$null.deviance - income.hts.2c$deviance) / income.hts.2c$null.deviance
pseudoR2_income.2c
```

#### 3f. Multivariate analyses refined - Income Change

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3c). This model includes the expanded (non-aggregated) income variable. It includes both race and families and children. 

```{r, echo=T}
income.hts.2d<-glm(disp_inc_change~1 +
                    income_var +
                    all_young_adults +
                    race_var +
                    children, 
                 family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                 data = hh_df)
summary(income.hts.2d)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_inc_change)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var +
                  all_young_adults +
                  race_var +
                  children,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(income.hts.2d)

confint(income.hts.2d) #confidence intervals
# coef(income.hts.2d) #log odds
exp(coef(income.hts.2d)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(income.hts.2d)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(income.hts.2d)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_income.2d <- (income.hts.2d$null.deviance - income.hts.2d$deviance) / income.hts.2d$null.deviance
pseudoR2_income.2d
```

##### 3g. Multivariate analyses refined - Income Change

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3f). This model includes the aggregated income variable (aggregated into 3 or 4 bins). It includes both race and families and children. 

```{r, echo=T}
income.hts.2d1<-glm(disp_inc_change~1 +
                      income_var +
                      all_young_adults +
                      race_var +
                      children, 
                    family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                    data = hh_df)
summary(income.hts.2d1)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_inc_change)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var +
                  all_young_adults +
                  race_var +
                  children,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(income.hts.2d1)

confint(income.hts.2d1) #confidence intervals
# coef(income.hts.2d1) #log odds
exp(coef(income.hts.2d1)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(income.hts.2d1)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(income.hts.2d1)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_income.2d1 <- (income.hts.2d1$null.deviance - income.hts.2d1$deviance) / income.hts.2d1$null.deviance
pseudoR2_income.2d1
```

# Model 4: Housing Cost

## Displacement - Housing Cost

### 2. Null Model - Housing Cost

```{r, echo=T}
# null model
housing.null<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1,family=binomial(link="logit"), data=hh_df)
summary(housing.null) #intercept value - same log odds for everyone to predict
coef(housing.null) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(housing.null)) #odds ratio of displacement: 0.38 to 1
exp(coef(housing.null))/(1+(exp(coef(housing.null)))) #probability of being displaced: 0.27
mean(hh_df$disp_housing_cost) #0.16
```

### 3a. Bivariate analyses - Housing Cost

#### Household income (5/3 bins)

```{r, echo=T}
table(income_var)

income.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + income_var, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(income.biv)
coef(income.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(income.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to households making \$100,000 or more, the odds of being displaced increase as income decreases -->

<!-- * $25,000 or less: ((2.88-1)*100) = 188 percent change in the odds or a 188% increase in the odds of being displaced compared to households making \$100,000 or more, controlling for the other income categories in the model, compared to 194% for displacement (4) -->

#### Household race (4/3 categories)

* AANHPI non-Hispanic, Hispanic, Missing/No response, Other POC non-Hispanic, White non-Hispanic
* AANHPI and Hispanic, Missing/No response, Non-AANHPI non-Hispanic POC, White non-Hispanic

```{r, echo=T}
table(race_var)

hhrace.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + race_var, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(hhrace.biv)
coef(hhrace.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(hhrace.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to white non-Hispanic households, the odds of being displaced decreases for AANHPI households and increases for other POC households -->

<!-- * Other POC non-Hispanic households: ((1.65-1)*100) = 65 percent change in the odds or a 65% increase in the odds of being displaced compared to white non-Hispanic households, controlling for the other income categories in the model, compared to 54% increase for displacement (4) -->

#### Previous housing tenure

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1prev_rent_own_bin2)

tenure.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1prev_rent_own_bin2, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(tenure.biv)
coef(tenure.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(tenure.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to households that own their homes, the odds of being displaced increases for renter households by ((3.67-1)*100=267) 267%, or a 267% increase in the odds of being displaced, compared to 162% for displacement (4) -->

#### Previous housing type

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1prev_res_type_simp)

housingtype.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1prev_res_type_simp, 
                     family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                     data = hh_df)
summary(housingtype.biv)
coef(housingtype.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(housingtype.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to households previously living in single-family homes, the odds of being displaced increases for households previously living in homes categorized as lower-density shared walls (Townhouse, Building with 3 or fewer apartments/condos) and higher-density shared walls (Building with 4 or more apartments/condos) - ((1.65-1)\*100=65) a 65% increase and ((1.88-1)\*100=88) 88% increase in the odds of being displaced, respectively. This compared to 52% increase and 58% increase for displacement (4), respecitvely.   -->

#### Housing transition

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1res_type_transition)

transition.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1res_type_transition, 
                    family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                    data = hh_df)
summary(transition.biv)
coef(transition.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(transition.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to no change, the odds of being displaced decreases for households transitioning to less space and higher density ((0.86-1)*100=-14) - a 14% decrease in the odds of being displaced, compared to 2% decrease for displacement (4) -->

<!-- * Compared to no change, the odds of being displaced decreases for households transitioning to more space and less density ((0.69-1)*100=-31) - a 31% decrease in the odds of being displaced, compared to 32% decrease for displacement (4) -->

<!-- * This may be related to lifecycle -->

#### Current RGC

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1_RGC)

rgc.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1_RGC, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(rgc.biv)
coef(rgc.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(rgc.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to households not currently living in the RGC, the odds of being displaced increases for households living in the RGC ((1.19-1)*100=19), compared to 32% increase for displacement (4). -->

#### COVID Moving

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1covid_moving)

covid.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1covid_moving, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(covid.biv)
coef(covid.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(covid.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to households who moved before COVID, the odds of being displaced decreases for households moving after COVID ((0.98-1)*100=-2), similar to displacement (4). -->

#### COVID Moratorium

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1moratorium)

moratorium.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1moratorium, 
                    family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                    data = hh_df)
summary(moratorium.biv)
coef(moratorium.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(moratorium.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to households who moved before the COVID moratorium, the odds of being displaced decreases for households moving during the COVID moratorium ((0.92-1)*100=-8) and -6% for households moving after the COVID moratorium. -->

<!-- * For displacement (4): -10% for households moving during and about the same for households moving after the moratorium. -->

#### COVID Moratorium

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1moratorium_binary)

moratorium.binary.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1moratorium_binary, 
                           family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                           data = hh_df)
summary(moratorium.binary.biv)
coef(moratorium.binary.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(moratorium.binary.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to households who moved NOT during the COVID moratorium, the odds of being displaced decreases for households moving during the COVID moratorium ((0.93-1)*100=-7), compared to -10% for displacement (4). -->

#### Lifecycle
##### Single person vs not

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1single_person)

singleperson.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1single_person, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(singleperson.biv)
coef(singleperson.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(singleperson.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

##### Single parent vs not

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1single_parent)

singleparent.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1single_parent, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(singleparent.biv)
coef(singleparent.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(singleparent.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

##### Families with children vs no children

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1children)

child.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1children, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(child.biv)
coef(child.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(child.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

##### All adults over 65
```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1all_older_adults)

older.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1all_older_adults, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(older.biv)
coef(older.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(older.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

##### Any adults over 65
```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1any_older_adults)

anyolder.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1any_older_adults, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(anyolder.biv)
coef(anyolder.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(anyolder.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

##### All adults younger than 35
```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1all_young_adults)

younger.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1all_young_adults, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(younger.biv)
coef(younger.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(younger.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

##### All people younger than 35
```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1all_young_people)

youngerpeople.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1all_young_people, 
                       family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                       data = hh_df)
summary(youngerpeople.biv)
coef(youngerpeople.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(youngerpeople.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

#### Vehicles/drivers

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$veh_drivers)

veh.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + veh_drivers, 
             family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
             data = hh_df)
summary(veh.biv)
coef(veh.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(veh.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Each unit increase in the vehicle/driver ratio is associated with a (0.83 - 1 = -0.17) decrease in the odds of being displaced -->

#### Education attainment max

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1edu_hh_max)

edumax.biv<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + f1edu_hh_max, 
             family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
             data = hh_df)
summary(edumax.biv)
coef(edumax.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(edumax.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

### 3b. Multivariate analyses - Housing Cost

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses.

```{r, echo=T}
housing.hts.1<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 + 
                     income_var + race_var + f1prev_rent_own_bin2 +
                     f1prev_res_type_simp + f1res_type_transition +
                     f1moratorium + 
                     f1single_person + f1all_older_adults +
                     veh_drivers,
                   family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                   data = hh_df)
summary(housing.hts.1)

arm::coefplot(housing.hts.1)

confint(housing.hts.1) #confidence intervals
# coef(housing.hts.1) #log odds
exp(coef(housing.hts.1)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(housing.hts.1)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(housing.hts.1)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_hts.housing <- (housing.hts.1$null.deviance - housing.hts.1$deviance) / housing.hts.1$null.deviance
pseudoR2_hts.housing
```

The model explains 8.5% of the variability in the data.

### 3c. Multivariate analyses refined - Housing Cost

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3b).

```{r, echo=T}
housing.hts.2<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 +
                     income_var + f1prev_rent_own_bin2 +
                     f1prev_res_type_simp + f1res_type_transition +
                     f1moratorium,
                 family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                 data = hh_df)
summary(housing.hts.2)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_housing_cost)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var + f1prev_rent_own_bin2 +
                  f1prev_res_type_simp + f1res_type_transition +
                  f1moratorium,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(housing.hts.2)

confint(housing.hts.2) #confidence intervals
# coef(housing.hts.2) #log odds
exp(coef(housing.hts.2)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(housing.hts.2)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(housing.hts.2)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_housing.2 <- (housing.hts.2$null.deviance - housing.hts.2$deviance) / housing.hts.2$null.deviance
pseudoR2_housing.2
```
The model explains 6.9% of the variability in the data.


#### 3c1. Multivariate analyses refined - Housing Cost

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3b). In addition, trying out single person hh or not. 

We're adding this additional test because of the colinearity between some of the lifecycle variables

```{r, echo=T}
housing.hts.2<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 +
                     income_var + f1prev_rent_own_bin2 +
                     f1prev_res_type_simp + f1res_type_transition +
                     f1moratorium + 
                     f1single_person,
                 family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                 data = hh_df)
summary(housing.hts.2)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_housing_cost)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var + f1prev_rent_own_bin2 +
                  f1prev_res_type_simp + f1res_type_transition+
                     f1single_person,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(housing.hts.2)

confint(housing.hts.2) #confidence intervals
# coef(housing.hts.2) #log odds
exp(coef(housing.hts.2)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(housing.hts.2)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(housing.hts.2)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_housing.2 <- (housing.hts.2$null.deviance - housing.hts.2$deviance) / housing.hts.2$null.deviance
pseudoR2_housing.2
```

#### 3c2. Multivariate analyses refined - Housing Cost

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3b). In addition, trying out all adults over 65 or not. 

We're adding this additional test because of the colinearity between some of the lifecycle variables

```{r, echo=T}
housing.hts.2<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 +
                     income_var + f1prev_rent_own_bin2 +
                     f1prev_res_type_simp + f1res_type_transition +
                     f1moratorium + 
                     f1all_older_adults,
                   family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                   data = hh_df)
summary(housing.hts.2)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_housing_cost)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var + f1prev_rent_own_bin2 +
                  f1prev_res_type_simp + f1res_type_transition+
                  f1all_older_adults,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(housing.hts.2)

confint(housing.hts.2) #confidence intervals
# coef(housing.hts.2) #log odds
exp(coef(housing.hts.2)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(housing.hts.2)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(housing.hts.2)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_housing.2 <- (housing.hts.2$null.deviance - housing.hts.2$deviance) / housing.hts.2$null.deviance
pseudoR2_housing.2
```

#### 3c3. Multivariate analyses refined - Housing Cost

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3b). In addition, trying out vehicles/drivers. 

We're adding this additional test because of the colinearity between some of the lifecycle variables

```{r, echo=T}
housing.hts.2<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 +
                     income_var + f1prev_rent_own_bin2 +
                     f1prev_res_type_simp + f1res_type_transition +
                     f1moratorium +
                     veh_drivers,
                   family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                   data = hh_df)
summary(housing.hts.2)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_housing_cost)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var + f1prev_rent_own_bin2 +
                  f1prev_res_type_simp + f1res_type_transition+
                  veh_drivers,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(housing.hts.2)

confint(housing.hts.2) #confidence intervals
# coef(housing.hts.2) #log odds
exp(coef(housing.hts.2)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(housing.hts.2)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(housing.hts.2)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_housing.2 <- (housing.hts.2$null.deviance - housing.hts.2$deviance) / housing.hts.2$null.deviance
pseudoR2_housing.2
```

#### 3c4. Multivariate analyses refined - Housing Cost

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3b). In addition, trying out race/ethnicity. 

We're adding this additional test because of the colinearity between some of the lifecycle variables

```{r, echo=T}
housing.hts.2<-glm(disp_housing_cost~1 +
                     income_var + f1prev_rent_own_bin2 +
                     f1prev_res_type_simp + f1res_type_transition +
                     f1moratorium +
                     race_var,
                   family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                   data = hh_df)
summary(housing.hts.2)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_housing_cost)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var + f1prev_rent_own_bin2 +
                  f1prev_res_type_simp + f1res_type_transition+
                  race_var,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(housing.hts.2)

confint(housing.hts.2) #confidence intervals
# coef(housing.hts.2) #log odds
exp(coef(housing.hts.2)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(housing.hts.2)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(housing.hts.2)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_housing.2 <- (housing.hts.2$null.deviance - housing.hts.2$deviance) / housing.hts.2$null.deviance
pseudoR2_housing.2
```


# Model 5: Community change

## Displacement - Community Change

### 2. Null Model - Community Change

```{r, echo=T}
# null model
community.null<-glm(disp_community~1,family=binomial(link="logit"), data=hh_df)
summary(community.null) #intercept value - same log odds for everyone to predict
coef(community.null) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(community.null)) #odds ratio of displacement: 0.38 to 1
exp(coef(community.null))/(1+(exp(coef(community.null)))) #probability of being displaced: 0.27
mean(hh_df$disp_community) #0.05
```

### 3a. Bivariate analyses - Community Change

#### Household income (5/3 bins)

```{r, echo=T}
table(income_var)

income.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + income_var, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(income.biv)
coef(income.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(income.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to households making \$100,000 or more, the odds of being displaced increase as income decreases -->

<!-- * $25,000 or less: ((2.88-1)*100) = 188 percent change in the odds or a 188% increase in the odds of being displaced compared to households making \$100,000 or more, controlling for the other income categories in the model, compared to 194% for displacement (4) -->

#### Household race (4/3 categories)

* AANHPI non-Hispanic, Hispanic, Missing/No response, Other POC non-Hispanic, White non-Hispanic
* AANHPI and Hispanic, Missing/No response, Non-AANHPI non-Hispanic POC, White non-Hispanic

```{r, echo=T}
table(race_var)

hhrace.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + race_var, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(hhrace.biv)
coef(hhrace.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(hhrace.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to white non-Hispanic households, the odds of being displaced decreases for AANHPI households and increases for other POC households -->

<!-- * Other POC non-Hispanic households: ((1.65-1)*100) = 65 percent change in the odds or a 65% increase in the odds of being displaced compared to white non-Hispanic households, controlling for the other income categories in the model, compared to 54% increase for displacement (4) -->

#### Previous housing tenure

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1prev_rent_own_bin2)

tenure.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1prev_rent_own_bin2, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(tenure.biv)
coef(tenure.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(tenure.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to households that own their homes, the odds of being displaced increases for renter households by ((3.67-1)*100=267) 267%, or a 267% increase in the odds of being displaced, compared to 162% for displacement (4) -->

#### Previous housing type

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1prev_res_type_simp)

housingtype.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1prev_res_type_simp, 
                     family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                     data = hh_df)
summary(housingtype.biv)
coef(housingtype.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(housingtype.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to households previously living in single-family homes, the odds of being displaced increases for households previously living in homes categorized as lower-density shared walls (Townhouse, Building with 3 or fewer apartments/condos) and higher-density shared walls (Building with 4 or more apartments/condos) - ((1.65-1)\*100=65) a 65% increase and ((1.88-1)\*100=88) 88% increase in the odds of being displaced, respectively. This compared to 52% increase and 58% increase for displacement (4), respecitvely.   -->

#### Housing transition

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1res_type_transition)

transition.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1res_type_transition, 
                    family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                    data = hh_df)
summary(transition.biv)
coef(transition.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(transition.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to no change, the odds of being displaced decreases for households transitioning to less space and higher density ((0.86-1)*100=-14) - a 14% decrease in the odds of being displaced, compared to 2% decrease for displacement (4) -->

<!-- * Compared to no change, the odds of being displaced decreases for households transitioning to more space and less density ((0.69-1)*100=-31) - a 31% decrease in the odds of being displaced, compared to 32% decrease for displacement (4) -->

<!-- * This may be related to lifecycle -->

#### Current RGC

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1_RGC)

rgc.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1_RGC, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(rgc.biv)
coef(rgc.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(rgc.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to households not currently living in the RGC, the odds of being displaced increases for households living in the RGC ((1.19-1)*100=19), compared to 32% increase for displacement (4). -->

#### COVID Moving

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1covid_moving)

covid.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1covid_moving, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(covid.biv)
coef(covid.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(covid.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to households who moved before COVID, the odds of being displaced decreases for households moving after COVID ((0.98-1)*100=-2), similar to displacement (4). -->

#### COVID Moratorium

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1moratorium)

moratorium.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1moratorium, 
                    family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                    data = hh_df)
summary(moratorium.biv)
coef(moratorium.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(moratorium.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to households who moved before the COVID moratorium, the odds of being displaced decreases for households moving during the COVID moratorium ((0.92-1)*100=-8) and -6% for households moving after the COVID moratorium. -->

<!-- * For displacement (4): -10% for households moving during and about the same for households moving after the moratorium. -->

#### COVID Moratorium

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1moratorium_binary)

moratorium.binary.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1moratorium_binary, 
                           family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                           data = hh_df)
summary(moratorium.binary.biv)
coef(moratorium.binary.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(moratorium.binary.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Compared to households who moved NOT during the COVID moratorium, the odds of being displaced decreases for households moving during the COVID moratorium ((0.93-1)*100=-7), compared to -10% for displacement (4). -->

#### Lifecycle
##### Single person vs not

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1single_person)

singleperson.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1single_person, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(singleperson.biv)
coef(singleperson.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(singleperson.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

##### Single parent vs not

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1single_parent)

singleparent.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1single_parent, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(singleparent.biv)
coef(singleparent.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(singleparent.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

##### Families with children vs no children

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1children)

child.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1children, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(child.biv)
coef(child.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(child.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

##### All adults over 65
```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1all_older_adults)

older.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1all_older_adults, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(older.biv)
coef(older.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(older.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

##### Any adults over 65
```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1any_older_adults)

anyolder.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1any_older_adults, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(anyolder.biv)
coef(anyolder.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(anyolder.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

##### All adults younger than 35
```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1all_young_adults)
# table(hh_df$all_young_adults, hh_df$prev_res_type_simp) # curious about how many under 35 were previously students living in dorms (table(hh_df$f1all_young_adults, hh_df$prev_res_type))
# table(hh_df$f1all_young_adults, hh_df$hhincome_bin5)

younger.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1all_young_adults, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(younger.biv)
coef(younger.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(younger.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

##### All people younger than 35
```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1all_young_people)

youngerpeople.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1all_young_people, 
                       family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                       data = hh_df)
summary(youngerpeople.biv)
coef(youngerpeople.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(youngerpeople.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

#### Vehicles/drivers

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$veh_drivers)

veh.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + veh_drivers, 
             family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
             data = hh_df)
summary(veh.biv)
coef(veh.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(veh.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

<!-- * Each unit increase in the vehicle/driver ratio is associated with a (0.83 - 1 = -0.17) decrease in the odds of being displaced -->

#### Education attainment max

```{r, echo=T}
table(hh_df$f1edu_hh_max)

edumax.biv<-glm(disp_community~1 + f1edu_hh_max, 
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df)
summary(edumax.biv)
coef(edumax.biv) #log odds (coefficients)
exp(coef(edumax.biv)) #odds ratio of displacement
```

### 3b. Multivariate analyses - Community Change

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses.

```{r, echo=T}
community.hts.1<-glm(disp_community~1 + 
                       income_var + 
                       f1prev_res_type_simp + f1res_type_transition + 
                       f1_RGC +
                       f1single_person +
                       f1children + f1all_older_adults +
                       f1any_older_adults,
                     family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                     data = hh_df)
summary(community.hts.1)

arm::coefplot(community.hts.1)

confint(community.hts.1) #confidence intervals
# coef(community.hts.1) #log odds
exp(coef(community.hts.1)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(community.hts.1)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(community.hts.1)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_hts.community <- (community.hts.1$null.deviance - community.hts.1$deviance) / community.hts.1$null.deviance
pseudoR2_hts.community
```

The model explains 2.9% of the variability in the data.

### 3c. Multivariate analyses refined - Community Change

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3c).

```{r, echo=T}
community.hts.2<-glm(disp_community~1 +
                       income_var +
                       f1_RGC,
                     family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                     data = hh_df)
summary(community.hts.2)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_community)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var +
                  f1_RGC,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(community.hts.2)

confint(community.hts.2) #confidence intervals
# coef(community.hts.2) #log odds
exp(coef(community.hts.2)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(community.hts.2)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(community.hts.2)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_community.2 <- (community.hts.2$null.deviance - community.hts.2$deviance) / community.hts.2$null.deviance
pseudoR2_community.2
```

The model explains 2.5% of the variability in the data.

#### 3c1. Multivariate analyses refined - Community Change

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3b).  In addition, trying out residential transition. 

```{r, echo=T}
community.hts.2_add<-glm(disp_community~1 +
                       income_var + f1res_type_transition +
                       f1_RGC,
                     family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                     data = hh_df)
summary(community.hts.2_add)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_community)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var + f1res_type_transition + 
                  f1_RGC,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(community.hts.2_add)

confint(community.hts.2_add) #confidence intervals
# coef(community.hts.2_add) #log odds
exp(coef(community.hts.2_add)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(community.hts.2_add)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(community.hts.2_add)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_community.2_add <- (community.hts.2_add$null.deviance - community.hts.2_add$deviance) / community.hts.2_add$null.deviance
pseudoR2_community.2_add
```

##### 3c1a. Multivariate analyses refined - Community Change

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3c), including residential transition. And also trying single person or not. 

```{r, echo=T}
community.hts.2_add<-glm(disp_community~1 +
                           income_var + f1res_type_transition +
                           f1_RGC +
                           f1single_person,
                         family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                         data = hh_df)
summary(community.hts.2_add)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_community)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var + f1res_type_transition + 
                  f1_RGC+
                  f1single_person,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(community.hts.2_add)

confint(community.hts.2_add) #confidence intervals
# coef(community.hts.2_add) #log odds
exp(coef(community.hts.2_add)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(community.hts.2_add)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(community.hts.2_add)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_community.2_add <- (community.hts.2_add$null.deviance - community.hts.2_add$deviance) / community.hts.2_add$null.deviance
pseudoR2_community.2_add
```

##### 3c1b. Multivariate analyses refined - Community Change

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3c), including residential transition. And also trying families with children or not. 

```{r, echo=T}
community.hts.2_add<-glm(disp_community~1 +
                           income_var + f1res_type_transition +
                           f1_RGC +
                           f1children,
                         family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                         data = hh_df)
summary(community.hts.2_add)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_community)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var + f1res_type_transition + 
                  f1_RGC+
                  f1children,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(community.hts.2_add)

confint(community.hts.2_add) #confidence intervals
# coef(community.hts.2_add) #log odds
exp(coef(community.hts.2_add)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(community.hts.2_add)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(community.hts.2_add)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_community.2_add <- (community.hts.2_add$null.deviance - community.hts.2_add$deviance) / community.hts.2_add$null.deviance
pseudoR2_community.2_add
```

##### 3c1c. Multivariate analyses refined - Community Change

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3c), including residential transition. And also trying all older adults. 

```{r, echo=T}
community.hts.2_add<-glm(disp_community~1 +
                           income_var + f1res_type_transition +
                           f1_RGC +
                           f1all_older_adults,
                         family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                         data = hh_df)
summary(community.hts.2_add)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_community)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var + f1res_type_transition + 
                  f1_RGC+
                  f1all_older_adults,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(community.hts.2_add)

confint(community.hts.2_add) #confidence intervals
# coef(community.hts.2_add) #log odds
exp(coef(community.hts.2_add)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(community.hts.2_add)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(community.hts.2_add)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_community.2_add <- (community.hts.2_add$null.deviance - community.hts.2_add$deviance) / community.hts.2_add$null.deviance
pseudoR2_community.2_add
```

##### 3c1d. Multivariate analyses refined - Community Change

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3c), including residential transition. And also trying any older adults. 

```{r, echo=T}
community.hts.2_add<-glm(disp_community~1 +
                           income_var + f1res_type_transition +
                           f1_RGC +
                           f1any_older_adults,
                         family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                         data = hh_df)
summary(community.hts.2_add)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_community)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var + f1res_type_transition + 
                  f1_RGC+
                  f1any_older_adults,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(community.hts.2_add)

confint(community.hts.2_add) #confidence intervals
# coef(community.hts.2_add) #log odds
exp(coef(community.hts.2_add)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(community.hts.2_add)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(community.hts.2_add)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_community.2_add <- (community.hts.2_add$null.deviance - community.hts.2_add$deviance) / community.hts.2_add$null.deviance
pseudoR2_community.2_add
```

##### 3c1e. Multivariate analyses refined - Community Change

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3c), including residential transition and all older adults. Also trying previous housing type.

```{r, echo=T}
community.hts.2_add<-glm(disp_community~1 +
                           income_var + f1res_type_transition +
                           f1_RGC +
                           f1all_older_adults +
                           f1prev_res_type_simp,
                         family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                         data = hh_df)
summary(community.hts.2_add)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_community)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var + f1res_type_transition +
                  f1_RGC +
                  f1all_older_adults +
                  f1prev_res_type_simp,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(community.hts.2_add)

confint(community.hts.2_add) #confidence intervals
# coef(community.hts.2_add) #log odds
exp(coef(community.hts.2_add)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(community.hts.2_add)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(community.hts.2_add)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_community.2_add <- (community.hts.2_add$null.deviance - community.hts.2_add$deviance) / community.hts.2_add$null.deviance
pseudoR2_community.2_add
```

##### 3c1f. Multivariate analyses refined - Community Change

This model includes all of the variables that were significant in the bivariate analyses and then refined to those significant in the multivariate model (3c), including residential transition and any older adults. Also trying previous housing type.

```{r, echo=T}
community.hts.2_add<-glm(disp_community~1 +
                           income_var + f1res_type_transition +
                           f1_RGC +
                           f1any_older_adults +
                           f1prev_res_type_simp,
                         family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                         data = hh_df)
summary(community.hts.2_add)

# to get frequency/proportion for each variable 
Hmisc::describe(as.factor(disp_community)~1 + #disp_binary needs to be factor otherwise summary stats are numeric
                  income_var + f1res_type_transition +
                  f1_RGC +
                  f1any_older_adults +
                  f1prev_res_type_simp,
                family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
                data = hh_df,
                na.action=na.omit)

arm::coefplot(community.hts.2_add)

confint(community.hts.2_add) #confidence intervals
# coef(community.hts.2_add) #log odds
exp(coef(community.hts.2_add)) #exponentiate to get predicted odds- odds ratio coefficients
```

```{r, echo=T}
BIC(community.hts.2_add)

# extract residual and null deviances
# objects(community.hts.2_add)

# calculate the pseudo-R2 value:
pseudoR2_community.2_add <- (community.hts.2_add$null.deviance - community.hts.2_add$deviance) / community.hts.2_add$null.deviance
pseudoR2_community.2_add
```
:::
